title: Unexpected grants required for materialized view source table
description:
### Company or project name
Cloudflare
### Describe what's wrong
When defining select grants for a materialized view, I have to grant access to source table columns that do not exist. I think this is best illustrated with a small test, which I would expect to pass:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
CURDIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
# shellcheck source=../shell_config.sh
. "$CURDIR"/../shell_config.sh
$CLICKHOUSE_CLIENT -n --query "
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS source_table;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS mv_table;
CREATE TABLE source_table
(
date Date
)
ENGINE = Null;
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_table
(
date Date,
number UInt32
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY date
ORDER BY (date, number)
AS
SELECT
date
FROM source_table;
CREATE USER user_${CLICKHOUSE_TEST_UNIQUE_NAME};
GRANT SELECT(date) ON source_table TO user_${CLICKHOUSE_TEST_UNIQUE_NAME};
GRANT SELECT ON mv_table TO user_${CLICKHOUSE_TEST_UNIQUE_NAME};
"
$CLICKHOUSE_CLIENT --query "SELECT * FROM mv_table;"
# fails with: Not enough privileges. To execute this query, it's necessary to have the grant SELECT(date, number) ON test_e0zwjr5a.source_table. (Missing permissions: SELECT(number) ON test_e0zwjr5a.source_table). (ACCESS_DENIED)
$CLICKHOUSE_CLIENT --user=user_${CLICKHOUSE_TEST_UNIQUE_NAME} --query "SELECT * FROM mv_table;"
$CLICKHOUSE_CLIENT -n --query "
DROP USER user_${CLICKHOUSE_TEST_UNIQUE_NAME};
DROP TABLE mv_table;
DROP TABLE source_table;
"
```
The column `number` does not exist in `source_table` so why do I need to add it to the select grant?
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Run the stateless test
### Expected behavior
The test should pass
### Error message and/or stacktrace
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE source_table (date DATE, value INT);
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_table AS SELECT date, value FROM source_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
GRANT SELECT(date) ON source_table TO user_test_fuzz;
key_bug_features: Granting access to non-existent source table columns
